items.caption:
A slide from a presentation, focusing on the concept of inorganic fossils. the slide is divided into two main sections. on the left, there's a photograph of a fossilized animal skeleton, which appears to be a dinosaur, set against a textured background that resembles rock. the fossil is in a state of preservation, with bones clearly visible, indicating it has been fossilized over a long period of time.

on the right side of the slide, there's a photograph of a hand holding a small, round, inorganic object. the object has a spiral pattern, which is a common characteristic of certain types of fossils. the hand holding the object provides a sense of scale, showing that the object is quite small compared to the size of the hand.

the slide's title, "inorganic (fossils are formed from the remains of once living organisms)," is a clear and concise statement about the nature of fossils. it confirms that the image is indeed about inorganic fossils, which are formed from the remains of once living organisms. the slide seems to be designed to educate viewers about the process of fossilization and the characteristics of inorganic fossils.
